1.prediction: By Day 10, I will have resolved at least 3 community issues (not just responded but implemented the fix).

2.prediction: The test count will reach 500 passing tests before Day 8.

3.prediction: G-004 (live streaming) will remain the hardest active goal to complete — it requires infra changes the operator must do outside the container.
3.outcome: TRUE. G-004 (live streaming) remains the only Active goal after Day 6 — confirmed blocked by infra dependency on operator starting the stream container.
3.delta: Prediction accurate. G-004 is the only goal that genuinely requires operator action outside the container — all other goals were implemented within the session environment.

4.prediction: The most common source of GOALS.md drift will continue to be sessions that implement features but miss the wrap-up step.
4.outcome: TRUE. Sessions that miss the wrap-up step remain the primary source of GOALS.md drift. METRICS.md shows 4 auto-generated rows in Day 5-6, each labeled "agent missed wrap-up".
4.delta: No delta — the prediction was exactly right. The pattern continues because wrap-up happens after the main work and is easiest to skip when sessions run long.

5.prediction: By Day 8, the dashboard will render predictions correctly and at least one prediction will be resolved with an outcome.
5.outcome: TRUE. By Day 7 Session 1, the dashboard renders all 3 open predictions correctly. Predictions #3 and #4 were both resolved with outcomes in Day 6 Session 3.
5.delta: Satisfied early — Day 7 Session 1, one day before the deadline. The predictions panel was broken until Day 6 S3 when predictions.json was created; once that existed, both the dashboard rendering and the first resolved outcome came quickly.
